Full-month extrapolation up from 5764 → 5991 (+3.9%). Daily pace (fully-populated days) steady at ~186/day.

glasswing_cves.source: Major update 2026-04-21/22: Mozilla disclosed that Firefox 150 (MFSA 2026-30) fixes 271 vulnerabilities identified during a single Claude Mythos evaluation pass. 41 of those 271 fixes were promoted to CVE-tier; 3 (CVE-2026-6746, -6757, -6758) are explicitly credited to Claude in the advisory. Sources: Mozilla Blog, Help Net Security, SecurityWeek, TheNextWeb, Slashdot, Engadget, Cyberinsider 2026-04-21/22. Anthropic/Carlini direct-attribution list updated accordingly.
glasswing_cves.notes: The 271 Firefox figure is Mozilla's internal characterization of 'vulnerabilities fixed as a result of a Mythos evaluation pass' — most do not meet the threshold for a public CVE (only 41 do; only 3 carry Claude credit in the advisory). Counter-argument: 271-to-3 means the headline overstates CVE-grade severity and is partly an artifact of Mozilla's internal triage. We are retaining 283 as a total 'Mythos-linked fixes' number while distinguishing it from the 6 explicitly-credited CVEs.

ms_patch_tuesday.source: Tenable blog, Krebs on Security, MSRC (value unchanged since 2026-04-14 release; some trackers list 164 due to methodology differences, retaining 163 as consistent with prior runs)
zero_days_actively_exploited: CVE-2026-33825 (Windows Defender 'BlueHammer' — PoC on GitHub since Apr 3 pre-patch)
ms_patch_tuesday.ranking: Second-largest Patch Tuesday ever; shy of October 2025 (167).

rhel8_errata.source: Estimate retained from prior run. stack.watch parent page continues to return only YTD aggregate. Red Hat security data API still 404s.
rhel8_errata.confidence: low — placeholder; true April count likely 20-30 but not precisely determinable from available sources

weekly_cve.observation: W17 Apr 20-23 is now 909 after Apr 22 back-populated from 18 → 355. If we ignore Apr 23 same-day (29, will grow) and use Apr 20-22 fully-populated (880 over 3 days = 293/day), extrapolating across 7 days gives ~2,050. Much more likely W17 final will land 1,400-1,700 after full back-population. W16 came in at 168/day — the slowest of the three measured weeks — so no inflection is visible yet in the first full week post-Glasswing. Too early to call either way; the typical 2-week back-population lag means mid-May is when we'd first see Glasswing-disclosed CVEs surface in bulk.

exploit_intelligence.summary: Metasploit: 0/14 watch-list CVEs have modules in rapid7/metasploit-framework. Overall maturity unchanged from 2026-04-22: 5 weaponized (KEV-listed or ITW-observed), 2 functional/PoC (Thymeleaf SSTI pair, n8n Ni8mare), 7 with no public exploit. No escalations detected this cycle. Counter-argument: absence of public PoC / Metasploit module is NOT absence of exploitation — state actors and advanced red teams use private exploits that are invisible to this survey.

google_news_hits.headline: Mozilla fixes 271 Firefox vulnerabilities found by Anthropic's Claude Mythos in a single evaluation pass
google_news_hits.source: The Next Web / Help Net Security / SecurityWeek / Slashdot / Engadget / Cyberinsider / Mozilla Blog / TechBriefly / CodeRoasis
google_news_hits.note: MATERIAL UPDATE. MFSA 2026-30 lists 41 CVE-tier entries total; three (-6746, -6757, -6758) are explicitly credited to Claude in the advisory. The broader 271-fixes headline number includes lower-severity patches that Mozilla did not promote to CVE assignment. This is the first MAJOR vendor disclosure that quantifies a Mythos evaluation pass at scale.

google_news_hits.headline: Anthropic investigating unauthorized access to Claude Mythos Preview via third-party vendor breach
google_news_hits.note: Discord-linked group used shared contractor accounts + API keys to access Mythos Preview via third-party vendor. Flagging for analyst review — this is a governance/security signal about Mythos Preview distribution, not a CVE. Potentially relevant if unauthorized users produced CVE disclosures outside the Glasswing participant set.

google_news_hits.headline: Anthropic Mythos shaping up as nothingburger
google_news_hits.note: Skeptical coverage piece. Counter-thesis worth tracking: if only a handful of CVEs are directly attributed, the Glasswing claims may be overblown. Flyingpenguin piece 'How 22 Vulns Became 271 or Maybe 3' is parallel skepticism.

Still flagged for analyst review. Unauthenticated path traversal in LogScale (HTTP-adjacent). CrowdStrike is a Glasswing participant. If no third-party credit line on CrowdStrike's advisory, remains a strong probable-participant-self-scan candidate.
Carried forward. Symlink escape in Claude Code sandbox. Anthropic is the principal. Local attack vector reduces HTTP-adjacency.

Companion to CVE-2026-6746. Same disclosure batch. Same recommendation.

projection_comparison.commentary: April 2026 extrapolates to ~5,991 — ~500 below the conservative May anchor (6,491) and ~2,000 below the aggressive May anchor (7,991). Extrapolation up from 5,764 → 5,991 overnight as NVD back-populated Apr 22 (18 → 355). Daily pace steady at ~186/day on fully-populated days. April is now tracking ~5% below March 2026 (6,304) and the gap is continuing to narrow as back-population fills in. If April finishes at or above March, it will be the first time April (historically lower) exceeds March YoY — worth watching over the next 5 days. Counter-argument: NVD publishing latency (4–8 weeks) means Glasswing-era CVEs from the April 7 launch largely haven't surfaced yet; first meaningful volume signal still expected in May–June.

counter_arguments: Mozilla's 271-vulnerability Firefox disclosure is the biggest single-event data point since the April 7 launch, but it does NOT move our broader CVE volume significantly because the vast majority of the 271 fixes (268/271) were NOT promoted to CVE-tier. This is simultaneously (a) a strong validation signal (Mythos can surface hundreds of defects in one pass) and (b) a methodological warning (headline vulnerability counts are triage artifacts; only explicitly CVE-assigned bugs compare cleanly to prior years).

CVE volume has NOT spiked post-Glasswing launch (April 7) through day 23: April extrapolates to ~5,991, still ~5% BELOW March's 6,304. Two reads: (a) NVD publishing latency is 4–8 weeks so Glasswing discoveries largely haven't appeared yet; (b) Glasswing disclosures are staged through coordinated vendor patches and will arrive in lumps tied to Patch Tuesday / vendor release cycles.

Daily publication rate has stabilized near ~186/day fully-populated. Today's +366 over 24h is a back-population artifact — Apr 22 alone moved from 18 → 355. The underlying pace is well below the aggressive +2-5k/mo thesis.
The March 2026 spike to 6,304 (+57.1% YoY) clearly predates Glasswing and is driven by CNA expansion, automated fuzzing, and broader disclosure norms — not AI discovery.

CISA KEV April additions flat at 22 for three consecutive days (catalog 2026.04.21 unchanged). Still pacing near Q1 average (~24/mo). KEV additions are editorial decisions by CISA, not purely volume-driven. Five of 22 April entries are legacy re-adds (2009, 2012, 2020, 2023 CVEs) reflecting catalog housekeeping.
Microsoft's 163-CVE April Patch Tuesday is notable but a single data point; October 2025 already hit 167 pre-Glasswing, so high-volume Patch Tuesdays aren't novel.

Weekly CVE data: W14 186/day, W15 231/day, W16 168/day (final), W17 partial ~293/day over fully-populated 3 days. Once Apr 23's 29 back-populates, the apparent W17 'acceleration' may look less dramatic. W16 — the first FULL week post-Glasswing — came in as the slowest of the three measured weeks. If Glasswing were already inflating publication volume, we'd expect W16 to be higher, not lower.

The Register's 'Anthropic Mythos shaping up as nothingburger' (2026-04-22) and flyingpenguin's 'How 22 Vulns Became 271 or Maybe 3' represent a growing skeptic camp that argues Anthropic's numbers are PR framings around a small set of real CVEs. Our data supports a middle position: the CVE-credited set is small (6) but growing, and the '271 Firefox fixes' number IS real (Mozilla confirms it) — the debate is about what kind of bugs those 271 are.

NP+DI candidate sweep: CVE-2026-40050 (CrowdStrike LogScale, CVSS 9.8 path traversal) remains the strongest probable-participant-self-scan candidate pending analyst verification of credit line. Counter-argument: CrowdStrike has a mature internal security team and could easily find this without AI assistance.

Exploit maturity on watch list unchanged: 5 weaponized (all already KEV-listed), 2 with functional/circulating PoCs (Thymeleaf SSTI, n8n Ni8mare), 7 with no public exploit. Counter-argument: absence of public PoC doesn't mean absence of exploitation — private exploits and state-actor tooling are invisible to this survey.

Mythos Preview breach (2026-04-21): Discord-linked group accessed Mythos via third-party vendor with shared contractor credentials. Relevant because if unauthorized actors are using Mythos, some bug disclosures attributed to 'unknown researchers' may in fact be Mythos-assisted — contaminating our probable-participant-self-scan attribution. Analyst should flag any suspicious recent disclosures against this possibility.

Zscaler surfaced as a possible new participant (per Zscaler corporate blog). Our config lists 13 participants but press coverage cites 'about 50 industry partners.' Analyst should verify and update participant list if confirmed — this would expand the probable-participant-self-scan search surface.
